description_long: This is a hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle with a natural grain and a darkened, worn appearance, suggesting frequent use. The handle is ergonomically shaped, thickening at the end for a secure grip and tapering toward the head. The axe head is made of dark metal, with a broad, wedge-shaped blade and a flat poll opposite the cutting edge. The blade has a single, straight cutting edge and a pronounced beard, extending downward for improved chopping efficiency. The head is securely mounted onto the handle using a traditional eye and wedge method, with no visible fasteners or modern attachments. There are no visible markings, engravings, or labels on either the handle or the blade.
description: A hand axe with a curved wooden handle and a dark, wedge-shaped metal blade. The handle shows natural wood grain and the blade has a single straight edge.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the curved wooden handle and the broad, dark metal blade with a straight edge.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the thickness of the blade and the ergonomic curvature of the handle's grip.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the handle's taper and the flat poll of the axe head.
description_view_3: Top-down view emphasizing the mounting of the blade onto the handle and the width of the blade's edge.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with ergonomic grip, natural grain, oval cross-section, and flared end.
attribute_blade_design: Single straight-edged, wedge-shaped steel blade with a pronounced beard and flat poll.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting through the eye of the blade.
